subject: Agricultural Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 0 CROP PRODUCTION
activities: Classifying different types of crops based on their life cycle (annual, biennial and perennial)
Learning Materials: Cabbage, onion, tomatoes. maize, groundnuts, cassava, lemons, oranges, pawpaw, mango, charts.
Put learners into small working groups of 4 -5.
Ask learners to research and prepare a presentation on the meaning of annual, biennial and perennial crops with examples.
Ask learners to report their findings.
AGRICULTURAL SCIENCE TEACHING MODULE FORM 2 TERM 1 &
resources: Classifying different types of crops based on their life cycle (annual, biennial and perennial)
AGRICULTURAL SCIENCE TEACHING MODULE FORM 2 TERM 1 &
Put learners into small working groups of 4 - 5.
assessment: A school agriculture club has started a garden demonstration to help learners understand plant growth and crop production. The club
intends to grow different crops, observe plant structures, and practice plant propagation techniques to improve crop production in the
community. As a learner with knowledge of crop classification, plant morphology, and plant propagation, you have been asked to
guide other club members on how plants grow and reproduce.
Classify crops according to their life cycles and give examples of each type;
Describe the external and internal morphology of plants, highlighting the main parts and their roles in plant growth;
